---
title: "Register-based research at DST"
subtitle: "Who is this guide for, and what will you learn?"
date: last-modified
---

This guide is for anyone working with register-based research at Statistics Denmark (DST).

---

## Where do you start?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4}
### New to register research?
Start at phase 1 and follow the phases in order - a little R experience makes the start easier.

→ [Start at phase 1](01_studieforberedelse.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Already know R?
Skip the introductory phases and go straight to the server, files and extractions.

→ [Jump to phase 3](03_log-ind-dst.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Working on DARTER?
Project-specific setup steps and guidance.

→ [DARTER - overview and pipeline](darter/00_index.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.callout-tip}
**Looking for something specific?** Use the search box in the top right - it searches across the entire guide.
:::

---

## SDS and DST

As a researcher, you work on **Statistics Denmark's (DST) servers**.
DST receives and processes data from, among others, the Danish Health Data Authority (SDS), which holds the raw national health registers (LPR, LMDB, cancer register etc.) and makes them available to researchers via a secure remote connection.

---

## The phases of the guide

The guide is built as 14 phases. The roadmap shows the natural path through, from planning to export; the table below gives a quick overview with links. You don't have to read everything in order - the look-up pages (functions, pitfalls, overview of registers etc.) can be visited as you go.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    S1["①  Get started<br>1 · 2 · 3"] --> S2["②  Get data<br>4 · 5 · 6 · 7 · 8"] --> S3["③  Build the study<br>9 · 10"] --> S4["④  Extract variables & assemble<br>11 · 12"] --> S5["⑤  Analyse & finish<br>13 · 14"]
    O1["Functions: overview"] ~~~ O2["Format tables"] ~~~ O3["DST pitfalls"] ~~~ O4["Overview of registers"] ~~~ O5["Good code practice"] ~~~ O6["Algorithms & special packages"] ~~~ O7["Learning resources"] ~~~ O8["DARTER - Project 708421"]
    classDef stage fill:#eaf2fb,stroke:#4a78b5,stroke-width:1px,color:#173a5e;
    classDef ref fill:#f6f6f6,stroke:#aaaaaa,color:#555555;
    class S1,S2,S3,S4,S5 stage
    class O1,O2,O3,O4,O5,O6,O7,O8 ref
```

| Phase | Contents |
|---|---|
| [1 - Plan your study](01_studieforberedelse.qmd) | Research question, key concepts and data model |
| [2 - R: the bare essentials](02_r-intro.qmd) | The minimum of R you need to get going |
| [3 - Log in to DST](03_log-ind-dst.qmd) | Access to the server and the first overview |
| [4 - File types and loading](04_filformater.qmd) | Parquet and SAS - formats and conversion |
| [5 - Extracting data step by step](05_universelle-moenster.qmd) | The universal extraction pattern: open_dataset → filter → collect |
| [6 - First extraction](06_foerste-udtraek.qmd) | Your first real extraction with synthetic data |
| [7 - Inspect your data](07_inspicer-data.qmd) | Check structure, types and distributions before analysis |
| [8 - Find your registers](08_kend-dine-registre.qmd) | Find the right registers for exposure, outcome and covariates |
| [Understand LPR](09a_forstaa_lpr.qmd) | LPR2/LPR3 and ICD codes - *9a understand · 9b extract* |
| [10 - Build your study population](10_byg-din-kohorte.qmd) | Cohort, index date, in/exclusion and censoring |
| [11 - Extract variables](11_foej-variabler.qmd) | Extract outcomes, socioeconomics and comorbidity - filtered to the cohort |
| [12 - Assemble and prepare the dataset](12_data-manipulation.qmd) | Joins, pivots and handling missing data |
| [13 - Analysis](13_analyse.qmd) | Tables, figures, regression and time-to-event |
| [14 - Export and repatriation](14_eksport-hjemsendelse.qmd) | Get your results safely out of DST |

### Look-ups - use as you go

These pages are **not** part of the numbered journey - jump here when you have a specific question.

| Page | Use it when you want to... |
|---|---|
| [Functions: overview](15a_guide_til_funktioner.qmd) | look up a single function - what do `filter()`, `collect()`, `%>%`, `left_join()` do? |
| [Format tables](15b_formateringstabeller.qmd) | translate codes to text (municipality, education and employment codes) with DST's SAS format files |
| [DST pitfalls](15c_dst_faldgruber.qmd) | check the errors that most often cost time and give uninformative messages |
| [Overview of registers](15d_register_reference.qmd) | find the confirmed column names, types and join keys for each register |
| [Good code practice](12_god-kode-praksis.qmd) | structure, naming and reproducible code |
| [Algorithms & special packages](14a_pakker-oversigt.qmd) | ready-made algorithms (OSDC, NMI) to derive variables |
| [Learning resources](15e_laeringsressourcer.qmd) | find courses, books and references for R, epidemiology and statistics |
